Resolution events:
- Crans-Montana bail protest resolved to Italy. The clue was old reporting that Giorgia Meloni called for stern punishment after Italian victims were identified. This reinforces using victim-country political reaction rather than only geography.
- Alcaraz's fourth-round opponent resolved to Tommy Paul. The direct draw path worked once Vukic/Opelka were removed, but earlier updates were underweighted in the score. Lesson: for live tennis draw questions, submit the cleaned direct-draw distribution as soon as the second-round pairings are set.

Updates made:
- Ukraine/Russia/US talks: Jan. 22 Moscow Times/Al Jazeera/Reuters-style reporting said Witkoff and Kushner would fly from Moscow to Abu Dhabi, where talks would continue in military-to-military working groups; Zelensky also suggested trilateral talks in the UAE. Moved the Feb. 4 second-round venue strongly to Abu Dhabi, with Moscow a smaller hedge.
- Australian Open women: the official third-round draw now shows Keys vs Karolina Pliskova and Pegula vs Oksana Selekhmeteva in the same mini-section. Moved Keys' eliminator to Pegula first, Pliskova second, with Oksana/Anisimova/Noskova tails. For Sabalenka's QF opponent, corrected the bracket: Paolini/Putintseva/Sonmez/Jovic are the relevant QF-opponent candidates, not Svitolina/Linette, so Paolini is now the clear leader.
- NFL: Sky/AP coverage framed the conference-title weekend as Seahawks/Rams and Broncos/Patriots, with Denver relying on backup Jarrett Stidham while Drake Maye and Matthew Stafford are MVP finalists. Kept Seattle first for the Super Bowl but raised New England and the Rams, and left the NFC QB market as Darnold narrowly over Stafford.
- Board of Peace/Gaza mission: the Jan. 22 membership list included Indonesia, Pakistan, Turkey and others. Raised Indonesia for the later Gaza mission troop-readiness question and increased Pakistan/Turkey behind it.
- Gaza children: Rafah crossing is expected to open next week, so I added Egypt as a material destination for the WHO-facilitated evacuation while keeping Italy first.
- UFC Hall of Fame: no direct announcement, but Ronda Rousey/Anderson Silva are already weak candidates as prior Hall figures in the real-world baseline. Added Dustin Poirier and Donald Cerrone ahead of them.
- Wildfire funds agency, Milan Olympics agency protest, tariff country, Indigenous-rights city, Sudan siege state, IAEA request country and AO men's title were re-submitted or modestly updated where state still showed stale priors.

Watched but no update:
- Tyson Fury comeback opponent, Bisan Owda platform ban, Netanyahu visitor, West African party dissolution, Greek factory explosion, Pat Cummins replacement, Epstein/Commerce Secretary, Crans-Montana skier crash and Champions League playoff draw markets had no Jan. 22 signal strong enough for a confident move.

## 2026-01-23 Update

Resolution events:
- UFC Hall of Fame first member resolved to Dominick Cruz. I missed badly by keeping Ronda Rousey/Anderson Silva in the mix despite weak real-world-baseline fit. Lesson: include recent-retirement UFC names such as Dominick Cruz and Dustin Poirier for first-announced Hall of Fame class slots; do not overweight already-Hall-or-obvious legacy priors.
- Wildfire recovery-funds agency resolved to FEMA, matching the agency lead. Lesson: disaster-recovery-funding misuse questions should default to FEMA unless the clue clearly points to HUD, OMB, DOJ, or a state agency.
- The second-round Ukraine talks venue resolved to Abu Dhabi. I had moved there late, but earlier snapshots still had Geneva/Davos. Lesson: once reporting says Witkoff/Kushner will continue with UAE working groups, submit Abu Dhabi explicitly and heavily; Davos can be logistics rather than the formal talks venue.
- Milan Olympics protest agency resolved to ICE, matching the agency lead. Lesson: when Italian protest wording says a U.S. agency's agents at the Olympics and domestic U.S. protest context is immigration enforcement, use ICE over generic DHS.

Updates made:
- Australian Open women: Jan. 23 results removed the stale Sabalenka QF candidates. Paolini and Tauson are out; Sabalenka next faces Mboko, while Iva Jovic faces Yulia Putintseva for the other relevant quarter. Moved Sabalenka's QF opponent forecast to Jovic first and Putintseva second. For Madison Keys, kept Pegula first but raised Karolina Pliskova materially after the draw narrowed to Keys-Pliskova and Pegula-Oksana.
- Australian Open men: Alcaraz crushed Moutet and looked sharper than the field in Jan. 23 reporting; de Minaur also looked strong. Nudged Alcaraz slightly ahead of Sinner for the men's title while keeping Sinner close and maintaining Djokovic/de Minaur/Zverev tails.
- NFL: Jan. 23 coverage confirmed the AFC final context of Drake Maye's Patriots against a Broncos team starting Jarrett Stidham for injured Bo Nix. Raised New England for the Super Bowl and kept the NFC quarterback market as Darnold narrowly over Stafford.
- Greenland/tariffs: new Davos coverage again framed the tariff threat around Denmark/Greenland, even with Trump/Rutte compromise reporting. Raised Denmark further.
- Australia Day/Indigenous-rights attack: corrected the stale Winnipeg/Ottawa city prior to Australia-specific cities led by Melbourne and Sydney, with Canberra/Brisbane/Perth tails.
- Sudan: kept North Darfur first but raised North Kordofan after Reuters-style Jan. 22-23 reporting emphasized al-Obeid and the widening Kordofan front.
- Gaza/WHO child evacuation and Gaza troop-readiness markets: kept Italy first for the child transfer while adding Egypt as a serious transfer-destination hedge; kept Indonesia first for a possible Gaza mission contingent and Pakistan/Turkey behind it.
- Greek factory explosion: no direct signal, but expanded beyond Patras/Thessaloniki to Athens/Volos/Larissa to avoid a too-narrow open-city forecast.

Watched but no update:
- Tyson Fury comeback opponent, Bisan Owda platform ban, foreign leader Australia-visit threats, Pat Cummins replacement, West African party dissolution, Netanyahu visitor, Epstein/Commerce Secretary, Champions League draw and Crans-Montana skier crash had no Jan. 23 naming signal strong enough for a confident move.

## 2026-01-24 Update

Resolution events:
- Madison Keys eliminator resolved to Jessica Pegula, matching the Jan. 23 draw-based leader. Lesson: once adjacent mini-section results are known, the direct draw path is worth moving aggressively.
- Sabalenka's quarterfinal opponent resolved to Iva Jovic, matching the Jan. 23 bracket correction. Lesson: after live tennis upsets, clear eliminated names immediately even when market aggregates remain stale.
- NFC Super Bowl starting quarterback resolved to Sam Darnold, matching the Seahawks-first forecast. This also means the Rams are dead in the Super Bowl winner market.

Updates made:
- Super Bowl: with Seattle confirmed as NFC champion, removed the Rams and redistributed mostly to Seattle, with New England as the AFC favorite because Denver is still relying on Jarrett Stidham for injured Bo Nix.
- Tyson Fury: Jan. 24 Sky reporting said Fury's April return is expected to be a lower-level comeback fight, with Fabio Wardley/Joshua-type fights more likely later in 2026. Moved the market away from Joshua/Usyk/Wardley and toward Arslanbek Makhmudov first, then Andy Ruiz, Jarrell Miller, Agron Smakici and Justis Huni.
- Australian Open men: Sinner survived but cramped badly in extreme heat and admitted he got lucky when the roof closed. Alcaraz remains the cleaner title favorite; Djokovic looked solid enough to raise slightly despite a foot/blister concern.
- Gaza children: Jan. 24 Reuters reporting again said Rafah crossing with Egypt is expected to open next week and Israel wants more people allowed out than in. Raised Egypt close to Italy for the Jan. 26 WHO-facilitated child evacuation destination.
- IAEA/Ukraine: no direct meeting-request report, but the substations/nuclear-safety story continues to point more toward Ukraine requesting a special watchdog meeting than Russia. Raised Ukraine slightly.
- Sudan: Jan. 24 had no direct siege-broken report. Kept North Darfur live but moved North Kordofan level with it because Reuters-style reporting has emphasized al-Obeid and Kordofan pressure.
- Greenland/tariffs: continued Greenland/Denmark coverage supports Denmark as the target country, even though the immediate crisis may be moving toward a framework deal.
- New Jan. 24 questions: for the Dnipropetrovsk miners-bus attack, kept Pavlohrad first but raised Marganets as a serious mining-town alternative; for the southern Lebanon abduction statement, raised Hezbollah and added Amal Movement as a small hedge.

Watched but no update:
- Indigenous-rights explosive-device city, Greek factory-explosion city, Bisan Owda platform ban, foreign leader Australia-visit threats, Pat Cummins replacement, Netanyahu visitor, West African party dissolution, Epstein/Commerce Secretary, Champions League draw and Crans-Montana skier crash had no Jan. 24 naming signal strong enough for a confident move.

## 2026-01-25 Update

Resolution events:
- Indigenous-rights explosive-device city resolved to Perth. I had Perth only as a small tail. Lesson: for Australia Day protest-security incidents, include Perth materially; large rallies are not only Melbourne/Sydney/Canberra.
- Sudan siege-broken state resolved to South Kordofan. I had South Kordofan live but behind North Darfur/North Kordofan. Lesson: when reporting mentions Nuba Mountains/South Kordofan under siege, do not let al-Obeid/North Kordofan coverage dominate the distribution.
- Tariff country resolved to South Korea. I missed entirely by overfitting the Greenland/Denmark tariff story. Lesson: tariff-percentage questions can be trade-policy/Asia stories even when contemporaneous geopolitics dominates headlines; include South Korea/Japan/China when the clue is a 15-to-25 percent import-tariff increase.
- Gaza child evacuation destination resolved to Jordan. I had Jordan as a tail behind Italy/Egypt. Lesson: WHO medical evacuations from Gaza often go to Jordan; keep Jordan near the lead when exact transfer counts are asked.
- Greek factory-explosion city resolved to Trikala. I missed by only using major/industrial-city priors. Lesson: Greek accident city markets can resolve to mid-sized regional cities; include Trikala/Ioannina/Lamia-style locations when there is no direct signal.

Updates made:
- Super Bowl: New England beat Denver 10-7 in the AFC title game, so the market is now Patriots vs Seahawks only. Moved New England narrowly ahead of Seattle, while keeping it close because the AFC game was ugly and weather-skewed.
- Australian Open men: Alcaraz beat Tommy Paul in straight sets and remains setless; Djokovic received a walkover into the quarters, improving his rest/fitness profile. Raised Alcaraz and Djokovic, lowered Sinner after the prior cramp/heat issue pending his next match.
- South Sudan displacement: Jan. 25 article headline says fresh conflict displaced 180,000, but full content was unavailable. Raised Upper Nile substantially because recent South Sudan displacement/conflict coverage most often points there, with Jonglei/Unity/Warrap/Western Equatoria tails.
- Gaza mission troops: Pakistan formally agreed to the Board of Peace/ISF and was reported willing to contribute 3,500 troops, so raised Pakistan behind Indonesia while keeping Indonesia first because the question's phrase is up to 8,000 troops.

Watched but no update:
- IAEA special meeting requester, Fury comeback opponent, Bisan Owda platform ban, foreign leader Australia-visit threats, Netanyahu visitor, West African party dissolution, Epstein/Commerce Secretary, Pat Cummins replacement, Ukrainian miners-bus attack, Lebanese abduction group, Champions League draw and Crans-Montana skier crash had no Jan. 25 signal strong enough for a confident move.

path=Path('articles/2026/01/26/articles.jsonl')
topics={
 'ao_men':['Australian Open','Sinner','Alcaraz','Djokovic','de Minaur','Zverev','Darderi','Fritz','Musetti','Learner Tien'],
 'superbowl':['Super Bowl','Seahawks','Patriots','Darnold','Drake Maye','New England','Seattle'],
 'market_fire':['huge fire at a market','market fire','Lagos','Dhaka','fire at a market','market blaze'],
 'manutd_carrick':['Michael Carrick','Manchester United','first defeat','Manchester City','Arsenal','Liverpool','Chelsea'],
 'gaza_troops':['Gaza','troops','8,000','Indonesia','Pakistan','Turkey','Bangladesh','Bosnia','military']
}
for topic,terms in topics.items():
 print('\\nTOPIC',topic)
 shown=0
 for i,line in enumerate(path.open(),1):
  try: art=json.loads(line)
  except: continue
  text=((art.get('title') or '')+'\\n'+(art.get('content') or ''))
  low=text.lower(); hits=[t for t in terms if t.lower() in low]
  if not hits: continue
  strong=False
  if topic=='ao_men': strong=('australian open' in low and any(x in low for x in ['sinner','alcaraz','djokovic','de minaur','zverev','darderi','fritz','musetti','learner tien']))
  elif topic=='superbowl': strong=('super bowl' in low and ('seahawks' in low or 'patriots' in low or 'new england' in low or 'seattle' in low or 'darnold' in low or 'drake maye' in low))
  elif topic=='market_fire': strong=('market' in low and 'fire' in low and any(x in low for x in ['lagos','dhaka','blaze','huge fire']))
  elif topic=='manutd_carrick': strong=('michael carrick' in low or ('manchester united' in low and 'carrick' in low))
  elif topic=='gaza_troops': strong=('gaza' in low and ('troops' in low or 'military' in low) and any(x in low for x in ['indonesia','pakistan','turkey','bangladesh','bosnia','8,000']))
  if not strong: continue
  shown+=1
  print('LINE',i,'HITS',hits[:10],'TITLE',(art.get('title') or '')[:160])
  flat=' '.join(text.split()); loc=min([flat.lower().find(t.lower()) for t in hits if flat.lower().find(t.lower())>=0] or [0])
  print(flat[max(0,loc-250):loc+1000]); print('---')
  if shown>=15: break
 print('SHOWN',shown)
PY"

- UFC Hall of Fame first inductee, wildfire recovery-funds agency, Gaza child evacuation destination, Pat Cummins replacement, Tyson Fury comeback opponent, Bisan Owda platform ban, Netanyahu visitor, Greek factory explosion and Gaza troop-provider markets had no Jan. 21 naming signal strong enough to move.

## 2026-01-22 Update

Resolution events:
- Crans-Montana bail protest resolved to Italy. The clue was old reporting that Giorgia Meloni called for stern punishment after Italian victims were identified. This reinforces using victim-country political reaction rather than only geography.
- Alcaraz's fourth-round opponent resolved to Tommy Paul. The direct draw path worked once Vukic/Opelka were removed, but earlier updates were underweighted in the score. Lesson: for live tennis draw questions, submit the cleaned direct-draw distribution as soon as the second-round pairings are set.

Updates made:
- Ukraine/Russia/US talks: Jan. 22 Moscow Times/Al Jazeera/Reuters-style reporting said Witkoff and Kushner would fly from Moscow to Abu Dhabi, where talks would continue in military-to-military working groups; Zelensky also suggested trilateral talks in the UAE. Moved the Feb. 4 second-round venue strongly to Abu Dhabi, with Moscow a smaller hedge.
- Australian Open women: the official third-round draw now shows Keys vs Karolina Pliskova and Pegula vs Oksana Selekhmeteva in the same mini-section. Moved Keys' eliminator to Pegula first, Pliskova second, with Oksana/Anisimova/Noskova tails. For Sabalenka's QF opponent, corrected the bracket: Paolini/Putintseva/Sonmez/Jovic are the relevant QF-opponent candidates, not Svitolina/Linette, so Paolini is now the clear leader.
- NFL: Sky/AP coverage framed the conference-title weekend as Seahawks/Rams and Broncos/Patriots, with Denver relying on backup Jarrett Stidham while Drake Maye and Matthew Stafford are MVP finalists. Kept Seattle first for the Super Bowl but raised New England and the Rams, and left the NFC QB market as Darnold narrowly over Stafford.
- Board of Peace/Gaza mission: the Jan. 22 membership list included Indonesia, Pakistan, Turkey and others. Raised Indonesia for the later Gaza mission troop-readiness question and increased Pakistan/Turkey behind it.
- Gaza children: Rafah crossing is expected to open next week, so I added Egypt as a material destination for the WHO-facilitated evacuation while keeping Italy first.
- UFC Hall of Fame: no direct announcement, but Ronda Rousey/Anderson Silva are already weak candidates as prior Hall figures in the real-world baseline. Added Dustin Poirier and Donald Cerrone ahead of them.
- Wildfire funds agency, Milan Olympics agency protest, tariff country, Indigenous-rights city, Sudan siege state, IAEA request country and AO men's title were re-submitted or modestly updated where state still showed stale priors.

Watched but no update:
- Tyson Fury comeback opponent, Bisan Owda platform ban, Netanyahu visitor, West African party dissolution, Greek factory explosion, Pat Cummins replacement, Epstein/Commerce Secretary, Crans-Montana skier crash and Champions League playoff draw markets had no Jan. 22 signal strong enough for a confident move.

## 2026-01-23 Update

Resolution events:
- UFC Hall of Fame first member resolved to Dominick Cruz. I missed badly by keeping Ronda Rousey/Anderson Silva in the mix despite weak real-world-baseline fit. Lesson: include recent-retirement UFC names such as Dominick Cruz and Dustin Poirier for first-announced Hall of Fame class slots; do not overweight already-Hall-or-obvious legacy priors.
- Wildfire recovery-funds agency resolved to FEMA, matching the agency lead. Lesson: disaster-recovery-funding misuse questions should default to FEMA unless the clue clearly points to HUD, OMB, DOJ, or a state agency.
- The second-round Ukraine talks venue resolved to Abu Dhabi. I had moved there late, but earlier snapshots still had Geneva/Davos. Lesson: once reporting says Witkoff/Kushner will continue with UAE working groups, submit Abu Dhabi explicitly and heavily; Davos can be logistics rather than the formal talks venue.
- Milan Olympics protest agency resolved to ICE, matching the agency lead. Lesson: when Italian protest wording says a U.S. agency's agents at the Olympics and domestic U.S. protest context is immigration enforcement, use ICE over generic DHS.

Updates made:
- Australian Open women: Jan. 23 results removed the stale Sabalenka QF candidates. Paolini and Tauson are out; Sabalenka next faces Mboko, while Iva Jovic faces Yulia Putintseva for the other relevant quarter. Moved Sabalenka's QF opponent forecast to Jovic first and Putintseva second. For Madison Keys, kept Pegula first but raised Karolina Pliskova materially after the draw narrowed to Keys-Pliskova and Pegula-Oksana.
- Australian Open men: Alcaraz crushed Moutet and looked sharper than the field in Jan. 23 reporting; de Minaur also looked strong. Nudged Alcaraz slightly ahead of Sinner for the men's title while keeping Sinner close and maintaining Djokovic/de Minaur/Zverev tails.
- NFL: Jan. 23 coverage confirmed the AFC final context of Drake Maye's Patriots against a Broncos team starting Jarrett Stidham for injured Bo Nix. Raised New England for the Super Bowl and kept the NFC quarterback market as Darnold narrowly over Stafford.
- Greenland/tariffs: new Davos coverage again framed the tariff threat around Denmark/Greenland, even with Trump/Rutte compromise reporting. Raised Denmark further.
- Australia Day/Indigenous-rights attack: corrected the stale Winnipeg/Ottawa city prior to Australia-specific cities led by Melbourne and Sydney, with Canberra/Brisbane/Perth tails.
- Sudan: kept North Darfur first but raised North Kordofan after Reuters-style Jan. 22-23 reporting emphasized al-Obeid and the widening Kordofan front.
- Gaza/WHO child evacuation and Gaza troop-readiness markets: kept Italy first for the child transfer while adding Egypt as a serious transfer-destination hedge; kept Indonesia first for a possible Gaza mission contingent and Pakistan/Turkey behind it.
- Greek factory explosion: no direct signal, but expanded beyond Patras/Thessaloniki to Athens/Volos/Larissa to avoid a too-narrow open-city forecast.

Watched but no update:
- Tyson Fury comeback opponent, Bisan Owda platform ban, foreign leader Australia-visit threats, Pat Cummins replacement, West African party dissolution, Netanyahu visitor, Epstein/Commerce Secretary, Champions League draw and Crans-Montana skier crash had no Jan. 23 naming signal strong enough for a confident move.

## 2026-01-24 Update

Resolution events:
- Madison Keys eliminator resolved to Jessica Pegula, matching the Jan. 23 draw-based leader. Lesson: once adjacent mini-section results are known, the direct draw path is worth moving aggressively.
- Sabalenka's quarterfinal opponent resolved to Iva Jovic, matching the Jan. 23 bracket correction. Lesson: after live tennis upsets, clear eliminated names immediately even when market aggregates remain stale.
- NFC Super Bowl starting quarterback resolved to Sam Darnold, matching the Seahawks-first forecast. This also means the Rams are dead in the Super Bowl winner market.

Updates made:
- Super Bowl: with Seattle confirmed as NFC champion, removed the Rams and redistributed mostly to Seattle, with New England as the AFC favorite because Denver is still relying on Jarrett Stidham for injured Bo Nix.
- Tyson Fury: Jan. 24 Sky reporting said Fury's April return is expected to be a lower-level comeback fight, with Fabio Wardley/Joshua-type fights more likely later in 2026. - Australian Open men: Sinner survived but cramped badly in extreme heat and admitted he got lucky when the roof closed. Alcaraz remains the cleaner title favorite; Djokovic looked solid enough to raise slightly despite a foot/blister concern.
- Gaza children: Jan. 24 Reuters reporting again said Rafah crossing with Egypt is expected to open next week and Israel wants more people allowed out than in. Raised Egypt close to Italy for the Jan. 26 WHO-facilitated child evacuation destination.

Resolution events:
- Indigenous-rights explosive-device city resolved to Perth. I had Perth only as a small tail. Lesson: for Australia Day protest-security incidents, include Perth materially; large rallies are not only Melbourne/Sydney/Canberra.
- Sudan siege-broken state resolved to South Kordofan. I had South Kordofan live but behind North Darfur/North Kordofan. Lesson: when reporting mentions Nuba Mountains/South Kordofan under siege, do not let al-Obeid/North Kordofan coverage dominate the distribution.
- Tariff country resolved to South Korea. I missed entirely by overfitting the Greenland/Denmark tariff story. United kicked off Carrick's second spell in interim charge with an exhilarating 2-0 home victory over Pep Guardiola's City in the Premier League on Saturday. His attack-minded side swept away the gloom hanging over the club, with second-half goals from Bryan Mbeumo and Patrick Dorgu underlining their dominance against a disappointing City whose title hopes suffered a significant setback. "It has been a good week - a big result, a big performance and a big uplift with the feeling inside the stadium," Carrick told reporters ahead of Sunday's clash. "It is finding a balance between getting the encouragement and confidence from the game and performance and keeping level-headed and keeping our feet on the ground. "We have got another big game coming up. One game does not make you a great team, but it gives us a great foundation to build on."

Although the 33-year-old has a one-year extension option, a team source said the club has chosen not to activate it. Casemiro arrived at Old Trafford from Real Madrid in 2022 for around 60 million pounds ($81.11 million) and played a key role in United's 2023 League Cup triumph, scoring in the final, as well as being part of their 2024 FA Cup win.

"We've got to ride it and use the emotion, you have got to use the energy and use the confidence," Carrick, whose team moved up to fourth in the table, said. "You've got to be humble enough to understand how we could just achieve these two big results. It doesn't just come easy, so we need to continue that and bottle it and use it again."
United had to dig deep on Sunday as Arsenal bossed the opening half-hour and led through a Lisandro Martinez own goal. But Bryan Mbeumo was gifted an equaliser and stunning goals by Patrick Dorgu and Matheus Cunha in the second half sealed a first United league win at Arsenal since 2017. "The goals were fantastic goals and some of the football was good, and we looked dangerous at certain times," Carrick, who won multiple league titles in United's midfield, said. "Other times we had to defend, but it was just a performance with a bit of everything, really."

The world would love to see a Spanish-language album win for the very first time — Bad Bunny was once again the most streamed artist, globally, on Spotify last year. There's also “GNX,” which deserves the victory, as well. But the 2025 ceremony felt like Kendrick Lamar's victory lap, and even with three rap albums nominated in the top spot, it's not clear whether voters will give him the recognition he deserves. LANDRUM: I understand the appeal of a breakthrough narrative here, especially with such a globally representative field.
